channel.title: GraphSys '26 on GraphSys
channel.description: Recent content in GraphSys '26 on GraphSys

item.title: Call for Papers
item.description:
Aligned with Euro-Par 2026, the premier European conference addressing all facets of parallel and distributed processing, GraphSys ‘26 is the Fourth Workshop on Serverless, Extreme-Scale, and Sustainable Graph Processing Systems. GraphSys is a cross-disciplinary meeting venue focusing on state-of-the-art and emerging (future) graph processing systems, with an emphasis on causality and temporal graphs.

The workshop is designed for researchers, practitioners, and students interested in sustainable graph processing. Participants from academia, industry, government research labs, and nonprofit organizations are encouraged to attend and contribute to the GraphSys discussions and collaborations.

item.title: Challenges
item.description: GraphSys '26 also features three high-impact challenges that steer research toward causal and temporal reasoning in parallel and distributed systems. We invite submissions that push the boundaries of the field – whether through foundational vision papers, the release of novel datasets, benchmarks, or rigorous academic and industrial advancements addressing our three core challenges. Extended abstracts (2 pages) addressing one or more of the challenges are welcome in addition to full and short papers.

item.title: Organizers and Committee
item.description:
Organizers

Jože Martin Rožanec

Jožef Stefan Institute, Slovenia

Jože Rožanec received his Ph.D. from the Jožef Stefan International Postgraduate School and performed post-doctoral research at the University of Twente. He is a researcher at the Jožef Stefan Institute. His principal research interests relate to machine learning (time series, graphs, causality, and applications), with a special interest in scalable and distributed systems. He has served as a committee member in multiple international conferences (e.g., ESWC, ISWC, ECAI, EuroPar). He is an ACM and IEEE member and a member of the ACM Europe Technology Policy Committee.

item.title: Program
item.description:
Program

The detailed program for GraphSys 2026 will be published once the workshop schedule is finalized (by July 24, 2026).

GraphSys 2026 will be co-located with EuroPar 2026 in Pisa, Italy. Workshop dates: August 24–25, 2026.

The workshop will run a single track with one keynote, paper presentations, flash presentations and posters for the three challenges (causal discovery in HPC and datacenter management; causal understanding of quantum uncertainty; causal hardware–software co-design), and one panel.

item.title: Keynotes
item.description:
GraphSys 2026 will feature invited keynotes from leading researchers and practitioners in graph processing systems.

Keynote speakers and titles will be announced as they are confirmed. Please check back later for updates.

item.title: Supporting Projects and Initiatives
item.description:
The GraphSys workshop is supported by the following organizations and projects:

Supporting organizations

HiPEAC

High Performance, Edge And Cloud computing network for researchers, industry, and policy makers in Europe.

Visit website

ADRA

The AI, Data and Robotics Association bringing together industry and research in AI, data, and robotics in Europe.

item.title: Previous Editions
item.description:
GraphSys ‘25

The GraphSys 2025 edition website is available at 2025.graphsys.com. The workshop was co-located with EuroPar 2025 in Dresden, Germany.

GraphSys ‘24

The Workshop website is available here. The accepted papers were published at the ICPE Proceedings.

GraphSys ‘23

The Workshop website is available here. The accepted papers were published at the ICPE Proceedings.
